description: Lock Triangulation Surveying to baseline-and-angle survey measurement solved with trigonometry, not Mercator-projection cartography.

checks.reason: Mercator projection is a map-projection technology, not the method prerequisite for triangulation measurement.
Triangulation uses angle measurements and triangle relationships to compute unknown positions from a baseline.
Trigonometry enables the computational survey method without being the whole instrument or map-production system.
The node is anchored to Gemma Frisius's 1533 proposal for using triangulation in mapmaking.
